name: Crumpets & Muffins (English)
recipeInstructions:
MUFFINS:
1. Wash, peel, and boil the potatoes.
2. Rub the potatoes through a colander.
3. Add warm water to the potatoes (just warm enough to touch without discomfort).
4. Dissolve yeast and salt in the mixture.
5. Stir in sufficient flour to make a moist paste.
6. Beat well in a deep bowl.
7. Cover with a clean cloth and leave in a warm place to rise.
8. When risen, turn out onto a floured table.
9. Divide into pieces about 2 ounces each, roll into round shapes.
10. Place on a wooden tray dusted with flour.
11. Cook on a hot plate (ensure the plate is hot).
12. Carefully transfer muffins one at a time using a thin tin slice.
13. Turn over and cook the other side.
14. Brush off excess flour, cool on a clean cloth or sieve.
TOASTING:
15. Split muffins open along one edge with fingers.
16. Place on a toasting fork and hold near a clear fire until one side is nicely browned but not burnt.
17. Turn and toast the other side.
18. Once done, split again lightly buttered on both sides, reassemble, and cut into halves.
19. Serve immediately on a hot dish.
CRUMPETS:
1. Follow the same steps as for Muffins, but use only half the amount of flour to make a batter rather than a sponge.
2. Cover and leave for 30 minutes.
3. Beat the batter well, cover again, and let rest another 30 minutes.
4. Repeat beating three times with intervals.
5. When ready, spread batter into crumpet rings pre-dusted with clean lard on a baking tin.
6. Cook until golden on one side, flip over with a palette-knife, and cook the other side.
7. Transfer to a clean cloth to cool.
Both muffins and crumpets should be served separately and consumed quickly after toasting. This process serves approximately 2 dozen items each.
